classes.title: Summer Intensive Part 2 July 13 - 17
classes.description: Annoyance 2026 Summer Intensives 5 day course - 6 hours per day with additional 2 hour workshops PART 1: The Annoyance Training Center teaches students how to develop and maintain a strong individual voice in their improvisation. These intensives will address the initiation of a scene; developing and maintaining strong characters; learning to hold on to your initial choices while at the same time, expanding on (heightening) the choices you have made; and learning to become deft at creating and weaving unexpected, character-based choices into your work. Annoyance classes challenge the conventional wisdom of the rules of improvisation, examining what’s behind them and how they came to be in the first place. Simply put, we make you the best improviser YOU can be. All of our intensive teachers are ensemble members who have learned and performed with The Annoyance, and thus have first hand experience with the way we approach improvisation. PART 2: Took the Intensive Part 1 and want more? Want to go further with your exploration of our philosophy? This is for you! With this intensive sequel, we go deeper into the teachings of The Annoyance, tackling things like context, group scene work, ensemble and much more. If you can only take one week, take part 1 . In The Annoyance Summer intensives, we try to cover all of the information in The Annoyance's AP1-5 program each day - AP1 on Monday, AP2 on Tuesday, and so on. These classes meet for 20 hours over 8 weeks. In these intensives, we're trying to cram that info into 6 hours over one day. A truly impossible task. So, part 2 was created as a way to convey more of the information we couldn't get to in part 1, while also allowing instructors to get deeper into concepts they're passionate about, knowing the students all have at least part 1 under their belt. PREREQUISITE FOR PART 2: Students must have previously completed an Annoyance Part 1 Intensive or have completed AP5 within The Annoyance Training Center SCHEDULE: Classes: Mon - Fri 10am-5pm with a 1 hour break for lunch (1-2pm) Optional Workshops: Mon - Thu 5-7pm Optional Showcase: Fri 6pm PART 2 WORKSHOPS: Advanced Improv with Mick Napier Solo Characters with Max Kantor Puppetry with Sam Locke Finding Your Place: Character In Show with Hitch*Cocktails Please Disregard Dates and Schedule Listed Below (Those pertain to our non-intensive classes)

classes.title: WORKSHOP: Party of One (Solo Improv Scenes) with Brad Pike
classes.description: Party of One (Solo Improv Scenes) with Brad Pike This workshop will meet Friday, July 17th from 5:30pm-7:30pm No scene partner? No problem! Learn how to create compelling characters with strong emotional points of view and build scenes through heightening, discovery, and play—all on your own. You’ll also explore how to support a solo scene without pulling focus, giving you the tools to turn a one-person scene into a fully realized world! Brad Pike brings his talent and knowledge of solo improv scenes to this workshop, which he has honed as a member of the Harold Team Devil's Daughter (which include solo improv scenes in each and every show) as well as in his recent acclaimed scripted solo character showcase, Dark Ride.

classes.title: The Plot Thickens - The Art Of Improvised Genre & Narrative (Saturdays)
classes.description: The Plot Thickens: The Art of Improvised Genre & Narrative Drawing on the process of High Stakes Productions’ long running genre shows – Hitch*Cocktails and Clued In – this class is designed to give performers the tools to strengthen their confidence and competence necessary for compelling cohesive genre-based improv. Students will have the opportunity to practice character work inspired by genre archetypes, create complex plot structures and decisively deploy genre tropes. Through thoughtful and poignant exercises, students will explore all the elements necessary to create a strong narrative without churning out unnecessary plot points. Once completed, students will be able to create their own genre based improv show with the skills to execute character and plot, deftly navigate long form narrative and easily twist storylines into satisfying endings. With The Plot Thickens: The Art of Improvised Genre & Narrative , the skills to conquer narrative and genre will be raised to ensure the stakes will be high! Following this workshop, students will be able to: Effectively create genre-specific characters utilizing archetypes and genre markers. Maintain character filters and point of view while navigating an advancing plot. Confidently identify a plot strategy informed by genre, themes, and story beats. Feel comfortable tying character and plot together into a compelling improvised story. THIS CLASS MEETS SATURDAYS 1-3:30PM JULY 18 - AUGUST 22 PLEASE IGNORE THE BELOW TERM DATES

classes.title: WORKSHOP: Hone Your Authentic Voice with Maureen Nolan
classes.description: Hone Your Authentic Voice with Maureen Nolan This workshop will meet Saturday, July 18th from 3pm-6pm Develop your unique voice and point of view in this workshop from stand-up comedian Maureen Nolan! Discover how to draw from your honest experiences to develop unique comedic points of view, and learn how to share this perspective with others so they can see the comedy, too! Great for stand-up comedians of all experience levels! Maureen Nolan is a stand-up comedian, producer, and event host based in Chicago, IL. She is a topical/anecdotal comedian but has often been described as an "alt comedian" due to being gay as hell. She produces and hosts two shows Hack Job every other week at Blind Barber and Slumber Party every month at the Lincoln Lodge. You have seen her comedy on the Bi Wives Club, Laura and Friends, Gems of Chicago, and on stages such as the Laugh Factory, the Lincoln Lodge, iO Theater, and the Second City. She hosts the Loose Four open mic every Wednesday at the iO Theater!

classes.description: The Invisible Clown – Clowning and Improvisation with Misha Usov This is a two-day workshop that will meet on Fridays July 31st and August 7th, from 9am-12pm. Learn to improvise like a clown, and clown like an improviser in this very special masterclass from one of the guest instructor Misha Usov, PRESS QUOTES ABOUT MISHA USOV: • "Smartly funny." — The New York Times • "Mikhail Usov's fishing boat idyll is a comic gem." — San Francisco Chronicle • "One of the world's best clowns." — Stuttgarter Nachrichten • "Ingenious comedy." — Frankfurter Allgemeine KEY HIGHLIGHTS & AWARDS: • 10-year Star of Cirque du Soleil (Created the iconic "Fisherman" character for the touring show 'Totem'). • Featured on The Tonight Show (USA), BBC (London), and Le Plus Grand Cabaret Du Monde (France). • Gold Medal Winner at the International Clown Festival (Ostend, Belgium). • Two-time Silver Medal Winner at the International Circus Festival (Saint-Paul-Les-Dax, France). • Author of the acclaimed book "The Invisible Clown" and founder of the Invisible Clown Institute.

classes.title: AP1 - Mondays with Jill Mueller
classes.description: AP1: Chicago Improv for Beginners AP1 is an all-encompassing survey course that will give anyone a solid foundation in improvisational comedy – whether you want to perform, direct, write, or just know more. New to improv? This class is for you. AP1 introduces you to Chicago's approach to improv by covering a wide variety of concepts:​ The rules of Improv and breaking the rules Group mind (the myth, the facts) How to say "yes, and" and when to say “no” Finding or Creating the game Long-form Short-form Writing via improv Direction (taking and giving) Object and environment work Introducing a show This class will culminate in an optional student showcase at The Annoyance on the final Sunday of the term. Prerequisite: None

classes.title: AP2 - Mondays with TBD
classes.description: AP2: Beginning Annoyance Philosophy - Initiations AP2 is an immersive examination of The Annoyance philosophy on, and approach to, improv. AP2 focuses on decisive initiations and a strong point of view. This class focuses on the first seconds of scenes and explores how they affect the rest of the scene. In AP2, you will cover: Personal responsibility in a scene − taking care of yourself and your choices Trusting instincts Creating/Finding “Your Deal” at the top of the scene Beginning character development Object work as inspiration Environment as inspiration The responsibility of operating without censorship. Individual feedback and tendencies This class will culminate in an optional student showcase at The Annoyance on the final Sunday of the term. Prerequisite: AP1 or sufficient background in improv. Please let us know in the comments section below where you have studied so that we may contact them to confirm.

classes.title: AP3 - Mondays with Jeff Quintana
classes.description: AP3: Scene Work AP3 is about taking that next step in scenes. While AP2 focused on the beginning moments of a scene, in AP3 students are challenged to play with more complexity and style, juggling it all within a scene while always maintaining their strong point of view. Aspects of this class include: Acknowledging the realities of your scene partner Scenic development: Finding the Highways and Bi-ways of a scene to extend its length Managing context without getting in your head (the who/what/where/when/why/etc.) Creating specific, surprising choices Playing with Opposites and Curve Balls Creating and playing with multiple patterns or games in a scene In-depth three person and group scene work Protecting and Playing the Freak Learning how to repair a scene that isn’t working Managing forms and style DaDa scenes (playing fiercely without needing to “make sense”) Justification: how to use it not out of fear but as a tool. Personal challenge and goals based on individual feedback Prerequisite : Students must have completed AP2 to take this course.

classes.title: AP5 - Mondays with TBD
classes.description: AP5 AP5 focuses on the creation of an original improv show inspired and devised by the ensemble. Exercises are tailored to help the improviser discover and strengthen their individual comedic improv voice and also on the “hows” of bringing this voice into an ensemble. The class also will delve into the improv rehearsal process and work with the concepts of ensemble building, collaboration, show pacing, and good rehearsal habits. Prerequisite: Students must have completed AP4 to enroll in this class.

classes.title: CRASS1 - Mondays with Hails Reeves
classes.description: CRASS1: Improv to Sketch CRASS (CReating Annoyance Style Shows) is a the next step in The Annoyance's process; using improv to create written material. In CRASS 1, students will take all of the improv skills they've honed in AP1-5 and together with their director, create a written sketch revue. Students will be assigned additional roles outside of actor/writer and may be responsible for costumes, props, etc. These classes are more intensive than AP1-5 and will require additional work and rehearsal outside of class hours. For over 30 years the Annoyance has specialized in using improv comedy to create full length plays and musicals. From Annoyance classics like Splatter Theater and Co-ed Prison Sluts, to more modern shows like Steamworks: The Musical and Live Laugh Blood, The Annoyance has enjoyed pushing the envelope of what plays and musicals can be. With this class, students will start the journey of turning their improv into written works, refining, planning, and creating a full sketch revue. Using Annoyance style improvisation to build a sketch Theatre Roles Technical aspects of the theatre Prerequisite: Students must have completed AP5 to enroll in this class Tech: Tech rehearsal for the shows is TBD (likely 10/11) Shows: This show will culminate in two (2) showcases - Sundays 10/11 and 10/18.

classes.title: IG2 - Wednesdays with Face Off Unlimited
classes.description: The Annoyance Training Center in Collaboration with Face Off Unlimited Present: Improvising Games II Mastering the art of game-based improvisation isn’t just about being funny, it’s about precision, adaptability, and commanding the mechanics of play at a professional level. Face Off Unlimited has spent years refining this craft, proving that game play isn’t just an aspect of improv, it’s a discipline all its own. This advanced course dismantles the outdated idea that structured game improvisation is only for beginners. Instead, students will develop the technical precision, spontaneity, and deep comedic instincts that make top-tier performers in this discipline unstoppable forces on stage. Through hands-on exercises and guided scene work, students will: Master the precision, nuance, and depth of game-based performance Improvise with structure without sacrificing authenticity or creativity Develop an audience-aware mindset to maximize engagement and surprise Refine performance choices through a professional lens Understand the business of game-based improvisation—and how to make it a career Whether you want to elevate your performance skills or position yourself for professional opportunities in high-level game play, this course will challenge and expand how you think about, and execute, improvised games. Levels: This course is divided into two levels. Improvising Games 1 (IG1) will focus on fundamentals of professional and high-level game play, "C to A" thinking, and introduce the Face Off Unlimited approach to improv. Improvising Games 2 (IG2) will delve deeper into the approaches introduced in IG1, while also focusing heavily on hosting, audience interaction and manipulation, and expanding the roster of improvised games mastered. Prerequisite: Students must have completed Improvising Games 1 Showcase: This class will culminate in an optional showcase on Saturday, October 3 - 5:30pm

classes.title: MI1 - Thursdays with Brody Lemke
classes.description: MI1: Scene Into Song Musical Improv I is an introduction to improvising songs, and the art of 'scene into song'. You'll learn how to sing structured songs, create catchy choruses that audiences remember, how to dance the same improvised dance move with seven other people, and how to express honest emotion while getting big laughs. This class will culminate in a one-night-only Musical Improv class show. YOU DO NOT HAVE TO BE A GOOD SINGER TO BE A GREAT MUSICAL IMPROVISER! All you need is a supportive teacher to help you through the first few anxiety-ridden moments. I was never in choir growing up, and didn't think of myself as a 'singer'--I heard the vibrato of Broadway voices and thought 'I'm DEFINITELY not a singer. 'But it turns out I'm a GREAT singer--I just needed to improvise a few hundred songs to get VERY comfortable with my own voice. So whether you're interested in musical improv, or just want to feel more confident with your own voice, this class is for you. Prerequisite: Students must have completed AP3 or at least two improv classes at any theatre

iO Improv Level 1: Mondays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24 – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 1 introduces students to all of the concepts necessary to become adept improvisers and performers. Students will learn how to build and apply the basic fundamentals of improvisation through a series of exercises, games, and storytelling. Level 1 is the appropriate starting point for the inexperienced student, but even those with some improv or theater experience will find value in the lessons of Level 1. The class will culminate in students learning and performing iO's signature piece, The Harold, one of the most sophisticated and rewarding forms of pure improv ever created. While 'Yes, And' is the guiding ethos in improvisation, students will go above and beyond that simple principle and learn how acceptance, agreement, honesty, connections, listening, and commitment will help them discover the truth in comedy. Our expert instructors foster a safe and joyous environment for students to play, experiment, and relish in the experimentation of improv. Level 1 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

classes.title: iO Improv Level 2: Mondays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 2 expands upon the principles explored in Level 1 and teaches students the skills to make strong, bold and committed choices to elevate their characters, scenes, and performances. This level focuses on the importance of creating something artful and beautifully compelling on stage, challenging students to be more than just talking heads in their improvisation. Students will be engaged in exercises that encourage them to use physicality, environment, and stage picture to inspire their improvisation and raise the status of their improv from simple games and scenes to high performance art. The principles of Level 2 separate iO's training center from other improv schools by focusing on bold decision making and group support. Students will work together to commit to each other's choices and elevate those choices, creating truly unique and inspiring moments of improvisation. Level 2 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

classes.title: iO Improv Level 3: Mondays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 3 redirects the focus back on to the essential building block of longform improvisation, the two person scene. The exercises in this level will help students slow down and find grounded real points of view and relationships to explore in their scenes. Rather than act like cartoon characters, Level 3 helps students understand that by listening and reacting to what someone is saying to them, they can create compelling and emotional scenes that will naturally evolve into comedy. Students will learn to develop the natural instincts that will help their scenes flourish and connect with audiences and will receive individualized feedback from their instructor about their strengths and opportunities for growth. 'Every line is golden and every detail is golden.' The principles of Level 3 help reassure students that they already have everything they need to create a great scene. Students will stop "inventing" and start truly improvising with guidance on how to respond with real emotions and committed character choices. Level 3 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

classes.title: iO Improv Level 4: Mondays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 4 synthesizes the lessons from all of the previous levels and allows students to begin creating longform pieces that are cohesive, thoughtful, and artistic. Students will learn a variety of classic longform pieces in an effort to understand how structure can inform their art. Each form taught will offer students an opportunity to learn how a form can be a motivator of creativity and authenticity, and not just a rigid structure to adhere to. The skills in this class will help students perform longer sets and will encourage true improvisational exploration of ideas, concepts, and thoughts–urging students to actually say something with their art. Learn the rules, obey the rules, then break the rules. Additional focus will be given to iO's signature longform style, The Harold. Developed and performed at iO for the last 35 years, the Harold is now taught and performed all over the world and is a sophisticated and rewarding form that lends itself to all styles of improvisation. Students will learn the intricacies of performing the Harold with the understanding that the form is just a launching point for inspiration and not a destination in and of itself. Level 4 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. Level 5 pushes students into a true ensemble mindset and challenges the groups to create their own unique longform piece. Inspired by their work in Level 4 and all the previous levels, students will work together under the guidance and assistance of their instructor to brainstorm and generate a new, unique form that will be performed on stage. This level allows for a lot of personalized notes and diagnostics from the instructor and really helps students grow as individuals while also operating in an ensemble state of mind. At the end of this 8 week course, the students will spend the following 8 weeks performing their devised piece on stage at iO every Sunday night. Following the completion of level 5 and their performances, all iO graduates will be eligible to audition for the House Team program at iO, allowing them to continue their education and perform on a coached ensemble with weekly rehearsals and semi-weekly shows, sponsored by the theater. View all available days Level 1 introduces students to all of the concepts necessary to become adept improvisers and performers. Students will learn how to build and apply the basic fundamentals of improvisation through a series of exercises, games, and storytelling. Level 1 is the appropriate starting point for the inexperienced student, but even those with some improv or theater experience will find value in the lessons of Level 1. classes.title: iO Improv Level 3: Thursdays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 3 redirects the focus back on to the essential building block of longform improvisation, the two person scene. The exercises in this level will help students slow down and find grounded real points of view and relationships to explore in their scenes. Rather than act like cartoon characters, Level 3 helps students understand that by listening and reacting to what someone is saying to them, they can create compelling and emotional scenes that will naturally evolve into comedy. Students will learn to develop the natural instincts that will help their scenes flourish and connect with audiences and will receive individualized feedback from their instructor about their strengths and opportunities for growth. 'Every line is golden and every detail is golden.' The principles of Level 3 help reassure students that they already have everything they need to create a great scene. Level 3 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. classes.title: iO Improv Level 4: Thursdays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days Level 4 synthesizes the lessons from all of the previous levels and allows students to begin creating longform pieces that are cohesive, thoughtful, and artistic. Students will learn a variety of classic longform pieces in an effort to understand how structure can inform their art. Each form taught will offer students an opportunity to learn how a form can be a motivator of creativity and authenticity, and not just a rigid structure to adhere to. The skills in this class will help students perform longer sets and will encourage true improvisational exploration of ideas, concepts, and thoughts–urging students to actually say something with their art. Learn the rules, obey the rules, then break the rules. Additional focus will be given to iO's signature longform style, The Harold. Developed and performed at iO for the last 35 years, the Harold is now taught and performed all over the world and is a sophisticated and rewarding form that lends itself to all styles of improvisation. Students will learn the intricacies of performing the Harold with the understanding that the form is just a launching point for inspiration and not a destination in and of itself. Level 4 is an 8-week course that meets once a week for 3 hours. At the end of the term, each class will have a short 10-minute performance demonstration open to the public. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. If you would like to inquire about paying via mailed check or Zelle to avoid the fee, please email classes@ioimprov.com Please double check all details before booking. We do not offer refunds after purchase- no exceptions.

classes.title: iO Improv Level 5: Thursdays from 7-10pm
classes.description: Session 5 runs from August 24th – October 18th with no breaks. Please select the start day that works best for you and that will be the day and time you have class every week. View all available days This level is the culminating class of the iO program and is followed by an 8-week run of performances on Sunday nights from October 25th through December 20th, for which you do not pay tuition. Level 5 pushes students into a true ensemble mindset and challenges the groups to create their own unique longform piece. Inspired by their work in Level 4 and all the previous levels, students will work together under the guidance and assistance of their instructor to brainstorm and generate a new, unique form that will be performed on stage. This level allows for a lot of personalized notes and diagnostics from the instructor and really helps students grow as individuals while also operating in an ensemble state of mind. At the end of this 8 week course, the students will spend the following 8 weeks performing their devised piece on stage at iO every Sunday night. Following the completion of level 5 and their performances, all iO graduates will be eligible to audition for the House Team program at iO, allowing them to continue their education and perform on a coached ensemble with weekly rehearsals and semi-weekly shows, sponsored by the theater. Convenience fees are added for purchases made via credit card. classes.title: Accepting the Moment: Using Improv in Clown w/ Julian Hernandez (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 14 students. For festival tickets, click here ! This is a workshop for folks who’ve taken at least 1 clown class AND 1 improv class. Why does clown feel so hard? When you’re improvising, you’re flying free. You’re working with your scene partner to make your team laugh and have no trouble discovering games. But now you’re preparing a clown bit, and for some reason, it’s not hitting like it was when you dreamt it up in your bedroom. In this workshop we’ll implement improv techniques to play directly with the audience and react to every moment in real time. We’ll uncover how to treat the audience like your scene partner so you can effortlessly make them laugh and ditch the rigid plan. Once we slow down, we can start soaring all over again. Workshop: Sunday, July 12th , 12 PM - 2:30 PM Where: BCC DogHouse, Studio 2 (137 Montrose Ave) Teacher: Julian Hernandez Bio: Julian Hernandez is a Cuban man based in Brooklyn, New York. He's taught improv and clown across NYC since 2015, having studied at iO (Chicago), UCB (NY), and FIZGIG (NY). He’s an alum of The Second City New York and even opened for Maria Bamford and Chris Gethard. You can see his audience-driven physical comedy across the city as Jooby or with his improv duo Havana Club, seeking to boost up other Latine Performers. Hell, they even let our boy cook on Search Party (TBS) and in the indie hit, Free Time. He's performed on UCB house teams and devised original clown shows for the New York Comedy Festival from 2022-2025, pushing the envelope with experimental clown. Professionally, Julian was the Managing Director of Brooklyn Comedy Collective from 2021 - 2023. Overseeing all theater and Training Center operations as the business reopened in the wake of the pandemic. He then went on to Production Manage at The Second City New York and currently operates as an Artistic Consultant for The Brooklyn Improv. Follow him on IG: @joobliander. Follow him in real life if you can. Testimonials: “I felt extremely supported. Julian went out of his way to encourage me and see strengths in us. I believe Julian brought his a-game and really paid attention to us as well.” “Julian is a 10/10 teacher, and there are so many things I could say here. However, I believe Julian's greatest strength is making every single person feel welcomed and excited about doing improv. I looked forward to every single class, and thought of it as a strong highlight of my week.” Read more student testimonials here . If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Breaking the Fourth Wall w/ Tej Khanna (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Each class is limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, click here ! This workshop will focus on the art of tastefully breaking the fourth wall and playing games with the audience while doing improv. We will focus on incorporating elements of clown and physical comedy to make the most of playing with a live audience in the room. Prerequisite: Completion of BCC Level 1 Improv or equivalent at any major comedy theater. Workshop: Saturday, July 11th, 11:30 AM - 2 PM Where: The BCC Pig Pen, Studio B (130 Graham Ave, Brooklyn) Teacher: Tej Khanna Bio: Tej Khanna is a New York based comedian. He has been performing improv for over ten years. His trio Chicken Big has been featured on America's Got Talent, SF Sketchfest, headlined Fun And Dumb Improv Festival and Oberlin College Improv Conference. He has taught improv at Oberlin College, NYU and The New School. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Clown Level 1: Welcome to Clown Town w/ Hannah Mitchell (July-August '26) (Monday)
classes.description: This class is limited to 12 students. Level 1: Welcome to Clown Town Clown. The Great Equalizer. "It's you, of course, the clown. And that's important." -Nader Farman Clown trains us to be fully present. Class curriculum includes exercises from Jacques Lecoq, Jean Taylor, Merry Conway, Nader Farman, Keith Johnstone and more, aiming to move us past ego and toward our shared humanity. Winter is coming. But class is joyful and warm! Come on out, try on the red nose. Recognize the universal shared experience that is clowning. This class culminates in a class show at the BCC Pig Pen. 7-Week Class: Monday, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 13th - August 24th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Saturday, August 29th. 8:30pm. BCC Pig Pen. Teacher: Hannah Mitchell Bio : Hannah Mitchell is a performer, theater-maker, and educator whose process is guided by the improvisational arts, especially clown, bouffon & being a human with a body. She regularly collaborates with various ensembles to make new works, including with her clown company, WüfPak , and the experimental theater collective, CHILD . She’s performed at venues around NYC including The Public Theater, The Brick, The Collapsable Hole, Lincoln Center, LifeWorld, The New Ohio, Theater Mitu, HERE Arts, Target Margin, and The Brooklyn Comedy Collective, among others. And regionally/internationally she’s perfromed at The Philadelphia Fringe Festival, Bard SummerScape, Princeton Seuls en Scène, & The Edinburgh Fringe Festival. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Clown Level 1: Welcome to Clown Town w/ Jeff Seal (July-August '26) (Sundays)
classes.description: This class is limited to 12 students. Level 1: Welcome to Clown Town Clown. The Great Equalizer. "It's you, of course, the clown. And that's important." -Nader Farman Clown trains us to be fully present. Class curriculum includes exercises from Jacques Lecoq, Jean Taylor, Merry Conway, Nader Farman, Keith Johnstone and more, aiming to move us past ego and toward our shared humanity. Winter is coming. But class is joyful and warm! Come on out, try on the red nose. Recognize the universal shared experience that is clowning. This class culminates in a class show at Eris Deep Space. 7-Week Class: Sundays, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, July 19th - August 30th, 2026 Class may end between 5:30 PM - 6:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Thursday, September 3rd. 7:00pm. Eris MainStage. Teacher: Jeff Seal Bio : Jeff Seal is a graduate of the Clown Conservatory at the San Francisco Circus Center and studied Decroux-based corporeal mime at the Hippocampe Project in Paris. He performs regularly with his two-man clown act, Jeff and Buttons, makes comedy videos with Bankrukt Productions and is one of the founding artistic directors of Cloud City Theater and Art space. His films and documentaries have been published by The New York Times, The Discovery Channel, AJ+, gothamist.com and screened at The Tribeca Film Festival. Testimonials : Jeff is an incredible coach and director. I loved Jeff's pragmatic teaching style. I think his best strength as a teacher was meeting us where we were with whatever ideas/bits we brought in and helping us figure out what was working or not (and how it would be different performing in front of the audience than in front of the rest of the class). Jeff put us in position to learn how the skills we had picked up in the earlier levels could be applied to an act that would work in front of an audience. Jeff's an inspiring director with tons of funny and creative ideas. He helped us develop our ideas into much stronger and tighter bits, with many more games than we would have thought to include, while helping us think about structure so that all those games came together in a cohesive way without getting confusing or going all over the place. He also has a great mind for comedic possibility and guided us to discover entire bits from even the smallest seeds of ideas. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Clown Level 2: Unearthing Your Clown w/ Chris Manley (July-August '26) (Tuesday)
classes.description: This class is limited to 12 students. In this class, you will learn that anything can be the clown's script. We will build on the skills learned in Level 1, leaning into the most vulnerable parts of yourself and heightening them to a ridiculous extent. You’ll learn that anything can be the clown’s script, lean into the most vulnerable parts of ourselves and heighten them. You’ll identify pieces of yourself and find ways to explore them that are both loving and silly. You will also learn about the status dynamics of the 1 and 2 clown and you’ll get to try on both identities. By the end, you will have the tools to begin building your own clown show. Through your relationship to everything, you are the clown. Prerequisite: Clown Level 1 at BCC or similar clown experience. 7-Week Class: Tuesday, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 14th - August 25th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Thursday, August 27th. 8:30pm. Eris MainStage. Teacher: Chris Manley Testimonial : “Chris helped each of us find our voices in the class and learn to create authentic relationships with the audience. Clown has made me better at performance in general including improv. I would highly recommend this class to anyone who wants to get deeper into clown technique, have more fun and fulfilling audience interactions, or wants to get ‘unstuck’ and out of their head while performing. Rock on weirdos!” "Chris was so great at pushing us in a way to help us grow as clowns. It was wonderful to see such noticeable growth among my classmates, all in an environment that felt incredibly safe, warm, and welcoming." "Chris is a beautiful teacher. He's knowledgeable, open, kind, generous, warm, intelligent. He creates a classroom where students are free to take emotional risks, and an ensemble forms." Bio : Chris Manley (he/they) is an American writer, performer and clown. He is best known for his character Buttons. He was nominated for a Lucille Lortel Award for his performance in "The Nosebleed," has performed with Cirque du Soleil and is honored to be listed in Le Petit Clown August Alphabetique alongside Abbott & Costello. He has performed at Lincoln Center, Ars Nova, The Montreal Just for Laughs Festival, the Upright Citizens Brigade Theater, The People’s Improv Theater, the Toronto Sketch Fest and the Chicago Sketch Fest. You can see him in several national commercials and award winning films. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Clown Level 3: Ensemble w/ Jeff Seal (July-August '26) (Wednesday)
classes.description: This class is limited to 12 students. You’ve started getting laughs as a clown but now how do you do it 6 days a week and twice on Saturdays for an audience that’s never even heard of clowning? In this class we’ll take the skills you learned in Clown 1 and 2 honing your clown persona and learn to create working acts as part of a fully realized show, collaboratively created by the ensemble. How does your clown solve a problem? How do you work as a company? What's the world you're going to set up with lots of clowns? You will further build your clown character by finding a costume that makes you look and feel stupid and start creating material by finding your own stupid clown logic. We’ll also explore “le jeu” and status in clown duos and trios, as well as building a thematic world that ties the whole show together. Come to class with ideas, costumes, props or anything that ignites your imagination. Students should be prepared to rehearse as they would in a professional ensemble process - with homework prepped outside of class and attending classes and the tech rehearsal on-time and present. This class culminates in two class shows on the Eris MainStage Prerequisite: Clown Level 2 at BCC or similar experience. 7-Week Class: Wednesday, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 15th - August 26th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Tech Rehearsal: Sunday, August 30th. 4:00p - 6:00p. Eris MainStage. Class Show: Sunday, August 30th. 7:00pm. Eris MainStage. Class Show: Tuesday, September 1st. 7:00pm. Eris MainStage Teacher: Jeff Seal Bio : Jeff Seal is a graduate of the Clown Conservatory at the San Francisco Circus Center and studied Decroux-based corporeal mime at the Hippocampe Project in Paris. He performs regularly with his two-man clown act, Jeff and Buttons, makes comedy videos with Bankrukt Productions and is one of the founding artistic directors of Cloud City Theater and Art space. His films and documentaries have been published by The New York Times, The Discovery Channel, AJ+, gothamist.com and screened at The Tribeca Film Festival. Testimonials : Jeff is an incredible coach and director. I loved Jeff's pragmatic teaching style, which really lends itself to the Level 3 Clown course. I think his best strength as a teacher was meeting us where we were with whatever ideas/bits we brought in and helping us figure out what was working or not (and how it would be different performing in front of the audience than in front of the rest of the class). Jeff put us in position to learn how the skills we had picked up in the earlier levels could be applied to an act that would work in front of an audience. Jeff is really good at helping people find the game in their bits - this class is a great next step after Chris’ level 2. Jeff's an inspiring director with tons of funny and creative ideas. He helped us develop our ideas into much stronger and tighter bits, with many more games than we would have thought to include, while helping us think about structure so that all those games came together in a cohesive way without getting confusing or going all over the place. He also has a great mind for comedic possibility and guided us to discover entire bits from even the smallest seeds of ideas. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Crafting Characters w/ Yoni Lotan (July-August) (Thursdays)
classes.description: Crafting Characters This workshop is limited to 10 students. Crafting Characters is the workshop for any comedian looking to hone their comedic character skills. Want to create a set for an SNL or JFL audition? Working on a solo show? Wanna post your character videos on TikTok and the rest? This workshop will teach you the skills needed to make your characters pop for both live performance and filmed content. This class will culminate in a performance at Eris Deep Space. Prerequisite : Completion of BCC Level 1 Sketch or Level 1 Improv. We will also accept students who have taken at least one full-length Sketch/Improv course at a major comedy theater (UCB, PIT, Magnet, Squirrel, Second City, iO, Annoyance, etc.) or have similar prior training or performance experience can begin at this level. Please write your prior experience in the form when registering. 5-Week Class: Thursdays, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 16th - August 13th Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. Class Show: Sunday, August 16th. 7:00 PM. BCC Pig Pen Teacher: Yoni Lotan Bio: Yoni Lotan is a veteran NYC improviser who’s made waves with his long-standing improv team, Duchovny. As an actor, he’s appeared in shows including Hacks, Russian Doll, High Maintenance & Marvelous Mrs. Maisel, films including Please Don’t Destroy: Treasure of Foggy Mountain and Rough Night. He’s also known for JFL: New Faces. Yoni brings years of experience to the table as instructor as well as his trademark enthusiasm and playfulness. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: FREE - Gimme That Queer Sh!t w/ Lou Gonzalez Jr. (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, click here ! Time to do improv with a bunch of queers and have a dang blast. Meet new queer folks and do absolutely bonkers improv. Hang with old friends while making new ones while doing fun free and affirming scenes! This free workshop is open to improvisers who self-identify as LGBTQ+. Workshop: Friday, July 10th, 5:00 PM - 7:00 PM Where: BCC Pig Pen Studio B (130 Graham Ave) Teacher: Lou Gonzalez Jr. Bio: Lou has taught improv for 13 years done it for 17 and been a queer for more that that combined. They have been the Academic Director at UCBNY coached talent for Nike, Spotify and Hulu taught abroad in Germany, Denmark, Paris, Amsterdam, and London. They even once did improv in their Mom’s apartment in the Bronx. Cultured Much?! Their teaching was even featured on NPR with them Stating “Taking Classes with Lou felt like more than just taking a class..I've found a space where it felt a little easier to be myself (with them), and it's helped me bring a bit more of myself everywhere” Read more student testimonials here . If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: FREE BIPOC Workshop. Use Your Voice w/ Kenny Park Yi & Noelle Gonzalez. (Saturday, July 11th) (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, click here! This workshop will focus on using your voice and creating space for yourself on stage, while staying attuned to the audience and your scene partners. We will work on ways of bringing your whole self into scenes that celebrates you and your experiences. Expect coached scene-work and plenty of performance reps! This class is completely free for BIPOC Improvisers, sign up while there’s still room. Workshop: Saturday, June 26th, 5:00pm-7:30pm Where: BCC DogHouse, Studio #1 (137 Montrose Ave) Note: As this class is free, we expect perfect attendance. If you register, and are not able to attend the class, please email classes@brooklyncc.com ASAP so we can get someone in the class off of the waitlist. Teacher: Kenny Park Yi & Noelle Gonzalez. Kenny’s Bio: Kenny Park Yi is an NYC-based comedian. Originally from North Carolina, he was inspired by Amy Poehler to take his first steps doing improv in college with North Carolina State University’s Comedic Improvisational Alliance (the CIA). He has since had the fortune of learning and playing at the Washington Improv Theater in DC, and the Brooklyn Comedy Collective + Upright Citizens Brigade in NYC. Kenny plays with improv team The Slaysians (an all queer, all Asian improv team hell yeah), co-produces Boba Gays (a queer Asian comedy variety show hell yeah) with Alex Kim, and co-hosts Spice Rack (BCC’s BIPOC jam hell yeah) with Noelle Gonzalez. His other hobbies include cheeseburgers, smizing, and gay. Noelle’s Bio: Noelle Gonzalez is a Brooklyn based comedian, educator, and community organizer. As a Mexican American transmasc, Noelle’s work focuses on creating artistic space for BIPOC and queer folks in the comedy scene. They produce Masc Mandate, a butch centered improv show & fundraiser for trans migrants and cohost the BCC Spice Rack alongside Kenny! Read more student testimonials here . If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Fix It or Make it Worse w/ Susan Messing (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: This class is limited to 40 students. ‍ For festival tickets, please click here ! It is time to finally get answers to those questions and problems with improvisation that you have thought about forever and just can’t crack the code. This class is pretty self explanatory. Part lecture, part get up and go, no question too stupid. Let’s break improv wide open, take rules out of the picture, and celebrate comedy again. If you’re in your head, this’ll rip it out. Perfect for people who have studied too much and learned too little. All levels. Workshop: Saturday, July 11th, 2:15 PM - 4:45 PM Where: BCC Dog House, Studio 2 (137 Montrose Ave) Teacher: Susan Messing Bio: Susan Messing graduated from Northwestern University with a theatre degree in 1986. She has been improvising ever since, creating curriculums, teaching and performing for the iO, The Annoyance, The Second City, DePaul University, The University of Chicago, and around the globe. Her critically acclaimed show, Messing With A Friend, which ran for eleven + years, is currently on hiatus as she wouldn't want anyone to potentially die simply from watching long form improvisational comedy. Now that she is fifty seven years old and in the middle of an international pandemic, she is questioning everything she has ever previously and fervently believed. Susan lives in Chicago with her fiercely smart, talented, and kind newly 18 year old daughter and their two stupid dogs and one moronic and whorish cat. She hopes you are doing ok and likes you tremendously. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here .

classes.title: Game But Also Easy w/ Chris Gethard (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, please click here ! Chris Gethard firmly believes that UCB style game is a great starting approach for any improv scene. That being said, he also firmly believes that game should be free of pressure and that comedy should never feel like math. In this workshop we'll examine game as a tool that can make your life easier, and discuss how when improv is really cooking, it should look and feel easy despite how hard it is to pull off. Let's be honest though - most of why you are signing up for this workshop is because this guy was a great teacher twenty years ago, barely ever teaches anymore, and this is sort of like learning about World War II by sitting down with someone who served on a ship in the Pacific. (Also if you sign up for this workshop just to roll your eyes at the idea of game and are too cool for it, please just don't sign up. I really don't have anymore time left in life for people with enough disposable income to sign up for improv workshops just to raise their hand every thirty seconds to argue like that one kid from elementary school). Workshop: Friday, July 10th, 3:00 PM - 5:30 PM Where: BCC Dog House, Studio 2 (137 Montrose Ave) Teacher: Chris Gethard Bio: Chris started performing at UCB in the year 2000, before many of the people reading this were born. He began teaching there in 2003 and wound up writing drafts of many of UCB's early curriculums and running the UCB school for a few years. Along the way, he coached many influential groups, taught many influential classes (including a class called () that a bunch of nerds say was a game changer), and worked hard to apply what he loved about the ethics improv to work on his experimental television show "The Chris Gethard Show". It is not an exaggeration to say that if you have taken an improv class in New York City, Chris probably taught someone on your family tree of teachers at some point. This is not meant to be a flex - Chris started very young and is now very old. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Improv Level 1: Intro To Improv w/ Andy Junk (July-August '26) (Sundays)
classes.description: Intro to Improv This class is limited to 16 students. First time taking an improv class? You’re in the right place. This class will introduce new improvisers to the value of saying “Yes, and” to every idea (especially their own) to become joyful, fearless, and bold performers. Through games and exercises this class will teach students to react emotionally and honestly, to trust your gut, to support scene partners and embrace taking risks. These skills are essential to successful improvisation! Whether you want to be a performer or try out a class to meet new people, this class is bound to boost your confidence and creativity in a safe and supportive environment. This class culminates in a performance for the public. 7-Week Class: Sundays, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, July 19th - August 30th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 5:30 PM - 6:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Sunday, August 30th. 8:30pm. BCC Pig Pen. Teacher: Andy Junk Testimonials: Andy is fully committed to helping you learn. His enthusiasm and focus during class helps you raise your own intensity and level of commitment so you come out of each class feeling like you grew as an improviser, teammate, and performer. The level of enthusiasm and focus Andy brought was infectious. He also gave very detailed notes on scenes and could feel the effort he put in was above and beyond to help you learn. Andy was really thoughtful in explaining all the different forms. As someone who isn't on a team, I always enjoyed class but struggled with shows, as I don't have as much practice editing and tagging in. He was really supportive in explaining everything I wasn't as familiar with, and allowed us to play around/go with the flow/not be too worried about sticking so stringently to the forms. Great at giving notes! If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

Taylor is the best teacher I could have asked for! King! No judgement, constant support, friendly and light hearted environment I really appreciated how seriously Taylor took the role of “teacher”. As an educator myself, his skills are very impressive. He was very professional yet provided an amazingly open and safe space for all of us. I can’t rate Taylor highly enough. A top top dude. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Improv Level 2: Free Your Mind w/ Hannah Rehak (July-August '26) (Mondays)
classes.description: Free Your Mind This class is limited to 16 students. Love what’s happening onstage and you’re bound to have fun and fearless improvisation. This class will help remind you what you love about improv, slap you out of whatever may be holding you back, and cut out the naysaying voices in your head. We will look at how we sometimes bail on our choices out of a fear of making the “right or wrong choice" and to instead approach improv with a "fuck it / love it" mentality (because improv is one of the most wonderful things in the world if you don't treat it too seriously). This class encourages your artistic freedom with methods to initiate scenes with an emotional point of view, to be specific and surprising in your choices, to support your scene partner, and to confidently navigate the vocabulary and concepts of long-form improv. This class will move quickly, keep you on your feet, and challenge you with personal feedback. This class culminates in a performance for the public. Prerequisite : Completion of BCC Level 1 Improv OR students who have taken at least one full-length course at a major comedy theater (UCB, PIT, Magnet, Squirrel, Second City, iO, Annoyance, etc.) or have similar prior training can begin at this level. Please write your prior experience in the form when registering. 7-Week Class: Mondays, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 13th - August 24th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Wednesday, August 26th. 7:00pm. Eris MainStage. Teacher: Hannah Rehak Bio: Hannah Rehak is a performer, podcast producer and filmmaker with over a decade of experience in improv and sketch comedy. Originally from Chicago, she trained at iO and The Annoyance, coached at CIC, performed regularly at Logan Square Improv, and understudied Second City's touring company and the ETC. Hannah currently works at various theaters in New York coaching and directing improv/sketch. She performs with Donna's Beef and Foster & Lawrence — and she produces a handful of shows including: Fuck It Film Festival , Mouth-to-Mouth and Smallprov. You can learn more about Hannah at hannahrehak.com , follow her on Instagram at instagram.com/hmrehak , or really get to know her vibe by checking out Spark here . Teacher: Maya Sharma Testimonials: Maya Sharma is the type of teacher that you would skip recess to have lunch with. Kind, smart, and funny as can be, she creates an environment that welcomes everybody and anybody. She’s made me a better improviser, a better listener, and a better collaborator in total. I heavily recommend taking her class! Maya's one of a kind style allowed me to let go and feel great just being myself! Maya provides an encouraging and safe environment to fail with joy. Her positivity is infectious. Maya is a wonderful teacher for people who deal with anxiety. She was so supportive of everyone’s “vibe”, she made it so safe to be weird and take risks which I deeply appreciate, and she identifies what makes everyone unique and interesting to watch on stage. Maya has a fantastic encouraging attitude. She makes sure to point out the good and the things that need improvement, but she's right there with you trying to make it work. You learn a lot and feel free to experiment because Maya won't try to knock you down - she wants to help you hone your skills while respecting your individual style. Maya is an incredibly friendly and approachable teacher and curates a very supportive vibe for all of her students. No question every felt stupid to ask about the class, our scenes, or improv in general. She also offers genuine and earnest feedback which demonstrates that she really pays attention to every single improvisers. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Improv Level 3: Advanced Scene Dynamics w/ Maria Randazzo (July-August '26) (Mondays)
classes.description: Advanced Scenes This class is limited to 16 students. This class builds on the skills in Level 2, teaching students to be versatile ensemble players who can build long-form pieces organically while keeping the show and audience's experience in mind. By layering more complicated improv techniques and awareness onto already fearless play, students will learn how to authentically ground characters so they feel believable and use curve balls to keep scenes surprising. We’ll explore the dynamics that make up three-person and group scenes and how to augment and balance multiple points of focus onstage. And we’ll learn to stay out of our heads while balancing it all. In addition to learning advanced techniques, this class will encourage you to lean into the areas you shy away from and own what makes you unique as a performer through personal notes and individual challenges. This class culminates in a performance for the public. Prerequisite: BCC Level 2 “Free Your Mind.” 7-Week Class: Monday, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 13th - August 24th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Thursday, August 27th. 8:30pm BCC Pig Pen. Teacher: Maria Randazzo Bio: Maria is a WGA Award-nominated writer, comedian, and actor. She was most recently a staff writer on the Emmy-nominated Apple Orignal series The Problem with Jon Stewart. She's a contributor for Reductress, and you can catch her performing sketch and improv around NYC. From time to time she also performs as a drag king named Sal MarscarBone who is horny for sandwiches. Prior to NYC, Maria was an actor and writer with The Second City in Chicago. She was a member of The Second City Touring Company and a cast member of the Second City revue "She The People." She also trained and performed at Chicago's iO Theater and The Annoyance Theater. Testimonials: She made such a comfortable, fun and informative space! Really passionate about helping people break through their limitations and offering constructive criticism!!! If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Intro to BCC Improv w/ Molly Ledbetter (FAD 2026 Workshop) [Friday]
classes.description: Each class is limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, please click here ! This Intro class teaches you what the BCC’s approach to improv is all about - how to say both FUCK IT and LOVE IT while performing. FUCK IT means you don’t worry about the outcome or result of your efforts. You are not judging whether you perform things correctly or whether you make mistakes. You are simply committed to doing what you are doing without worrying whether it is good or not. LOVE IT means you celebrate the whimsy, joy, and delight inherent in making shit up onstage. You remember why you fell in love with improv, one of the most wonderful feelings in the world. Learn to hold both of these ideas in your head at the same time and create fearless, fun, and dumb improv. If you are new to improv, this workshop will set you up for a fearless way to begin learning. If you are an experienced improviser, this workshop will introduce you to a new philosophy of improv that is different than other improv schools in NYC. Either way you will be challenged and motivated! Drop-in Classes: Friday, July 10th, 5:00 PM - 7:00 PM Where: BCC DogHouse, Studio 1 (137 Montrose Ave) Teacher: Molly Ledbetter Testimonials: Molly was so phenomenal in every way. She was approachable, kind, warm, directive when needed yet always encouraging and amazing at making the class feel like a safe space. She was so vivacious and filled the class with so much energy yet created space for everyone to shine. Molly really listened and observed. She was really positive and had a ton of energy. She gave great feedback and was always constructive and encouraging. Molly is so warm and knowledgeable. If a class is sold out, register for the wait list here .

classes.title: Intro to Clown w/ Tallie Medel (FAD 2026 Workshop) [Saturday]
classes.description: Each class is limited to 12 students. ‍ ‍ For festival tickets, please click here ! "It's you, of course, the clown. And that's important." -Nader Farman What happens in a clown class? We introduce ourselves, we have a nice long warmup, our feet get squishy and our heads get floaty, we play with tennis balls and hats, we do group games and solo exercises, and we leave room at the end for questions. It’s active and exciting and my face hurts from laughing every time. You’ll probably leave with a new friend. We're in an era of cognitive dissonance, hence all this clown talk. Absurd times call for an absurd response. Clown is antifascist and you are brave! All experience levels welcome. classes.title: Intro to the Improvised Play w/ Nicole Asava (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, click here ! Improvise: to make, invent, or arrange offhand. Play: The stage representation of an action or story. In long form improvisation we often have only a couple minutes to get to know and share our characters with an audience, leaving no time for subtlety or nuance. In plays they have tons of time, some might say too much time. In this Introduction to The Improvised Play workshop, inspired by the class first developed by Brandon Gardner at UCB NY, we’ll combine the two, exploring the pivotal first scene that lays the groundwork for the rest of the improvised play. Whether you’re a trained actor who wants to explore slower improvisation, or an experienced improviser who wants to develop your acting skills, or both! Welcome to The Improvised Play! classes.title: Marrying Game & Deal w/ Philip Markle and Akmal Tajihan (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, please click here ! Combine multiple philosophies to become a better improviser! Giving yourself a well-rounded improv education by taking classes at different institutions is critical in growing your talent, education, and joy. But sometimes it is difficult to figure out how to combine these philosophies together into a successful scene. That is what this class is all about! Learn to bring the superpowers of different schools into one powerful set. We will focus on pairing the BCC’s f*ck it / love it philosophy and playing from a deal with a game-based approach to finding and playing with the unusual thing. We will cover core BCC concepts like managing surprise + believability & byways and highways with game-based methodology of “the voice of reason” and mapping beats out over a long-form show. The class will culminate in a simplified longform Harold. Learn to play fearlessly from a deal while honing the game of the scene - and keeping it all loose and fun. Philip trained in improv in Chicago at The Annoyance, iO, and Second City before moving to NYC in 2013 to launch The Annoyance Theater NY, where he acted as its Director for four years before founding the BCC. He was named “Top 5 Queer Business Leaders” by Time Out NY in 2023 and a “Power Player in Arts in Culture” by AMNY in 2024. The New York Times wrote, “Philip Markle, who founded [the BCC] in 2018, has done creative work building an audience around a dizzying array of shows.” He has taught thousands of students of all ages internationally, from Bali to Berlin, for over 15 years. Performance highlights include musically improvising with Baby Wants Candy in Chicago and BLANK: The Musical Off-Broadway. As a storyteller, he has performed at The Moth and The Paper Machete - and is a writer on Medium.com with 400k+ reads. He executive produced, wrote, and acted in the digital shorts series "The Annoyance Presents" on IFC's Comedy Crib. Philip first performed his show SPARKLE HOUR to rave reviews in the 2013 NY International Fringe Festival and has regularly produced it since, including multiple runs at Joe's Pub and as part of the 2021-2023 NY Comedy Festivals. You can listen to his queer comedy songs on your favorite streaming music platform under the name “Philip Sparkle.” Akmal’s Bio: Akmal Tajihan is an NYC Comedian and Improv teacher. He has served as a faculty member at Upright Citizens Brigade Theater teaching for the past 2 years and has been doing improv/ comedy for over 10. classes.title: Musical Improv Level 1: Structure w/ Daniel Tepper (July-August '26) (Tuesdays)
classes.description: This class is limited to 16 students. Structure (Gather Your Ingredients) Musical improv classes at BCC are taught at an accelerated pace compared to other schools. You will be performing a fully fledged musical by the end of level 1. You already know how to improvise, but to music? YES YOU CAN! This class will introduce improvisers with at least 1 level of experience how to create fully formed narrative musicals on the spot. We will begin each class with various rhythmic, rhyming, and listening, exercises, then delve deep into each element of narrative structure. Students will learn how to create strong protagonists, antagonists, charm scenes, and finales, while building a toolbox of musical techniques including fugues, and of course classic chorus-verse structures. Strong singing voices are not required! Just a willingness to unleash your inner diva in a joyful and supportive atmosphere. This class culminates in a performance for the public. Prerequisite : Completion of BCC Level 1 Improv OR students who have taken at least one full-length course at a major comedy theater (UCB, PIT, Magnet, Squirrel, Second City, iO, Annoyance, etc.) or have similar prior training can begin at this level. classes.title: Sketch: Level 1 w/ Micah Sterenberg (July-August '26) (Tuesdays)
classes.description: The Building Blocks of Sketch This class is limited to 12 students. This class is for anyone interested in learning the process of writing sketch comedy. Throughout the seven-week course, we will discuss, practice, and polish scenes that will culminate in a staged show. Each week will focus on one specific part of the sketch comedy world. These include creating through improv, finding the “game” of a sketch, writing characters, and more! Why do we love certain sketches? How does one go about writing a character? How do we create something from a blank page? Questions like these will be explored in a positive, low stakes/high fun environment. There is no magic key to unlocking great sketch comedy, but this introductory class will help beginning writers find some doors and start knocking. 7-Week Class: Tuesdays, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 14th - August 25th, 2026 Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Tech Rehearsal: Sunday, August 30th. 4:00pm - 6:00pm. BCC Pig Pen. Class Show: Sunday, August 30th. 7:00pm. BCC Pig Pen. Teacher: Micah Sterenberg Bio: Micah started improvising over a decade ago in Chicago and is a veteran of Second City, Annoyance, and iO, where he was a member of house teams Meridian and Tone. He's in the new season of That Damn Michael Che and was the creator of the long-running live show Bit Tank and the cult-hit Global Influencer SummitCon. He can be seen improvising at the BCC with WMYL and Olestra. Testimonial: Micah did an excellent job teaching the structure and rhythm of sketch and preparing us for our show! Micah has a really strong sense that I trust for what’s interesting, what is good, and what to do to get there. I thought when he gave constructive feedback to build towards those high level goals that was always really illuminating. Micah seems like a nice and cool dude and provided a great intro to the vibe of the BCC community. Our class was full of great folks, and I feel like Micah helped foster a safe and welcoming group dynamic. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Slapstick: Physical Comedy in Clowning & Improv w Anya Opshinsky (Saturday, July 18th, 2026)
classes.description: Each class is limited to 14 students. Slapstick: Physical Comedy in Clowning We’ll explore the craft of slapstick comedy with a focus on precision, timing, and physical clarity. We’ll work on how character fuels comedy, character matching, character dissonance - The “Straight Man” and The “Fool”. We’ll also cover tried-and-true slapstick classics like the double take, convincing trips, the invisible chair fall, chases, and well-timed collisions. All experience levels welcome. Drop-in Class: Saturday, July 18th, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM. Where: BCC Pig Pen (144 Boerum Street, Brooklyn) Teacher: Anya Opshinsky Bio: Anya Opshinsky is a German American mime, actor, director, improvisor, physical theater creator and teacher. Originally from NYC Anya moved to Paris to study physical theater at École Jacques Lecoq 8 years ago and has been creating, teaching and performing there ever since. Anya is the co-founder of Shift Shape Theater through which she creates and tours original shows the most recent of which is entitled "ROVERS: Two Mimes on the Run" which is currently touring around France. Anya has taught mime, mask-making, clown, physical theater and improv with HUGE Improv, MN, Vis à Vis Théâtre, Rise Comedy, Denver and Théâtre de Verre, Paris. She is the creator and producer of The Ultimate Set-Up that performs in Denver, Paris and NYC and works as a solo artist performing original drag, mask, burlesque and clown pieces in the Parisian cabaret circuit If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here .

classes.title: Stand-Up Level 1: Finding Your Voice w/ Sara Hennessey (July-August '26) (Sundays)
classes.description: Stand-Up: Finding Your Voice Class limited to 12 students. Have you always wanted to perform stand-up but don't know where to begin? In this class, we will breakdown how to get started writing a stand-up routine and find your voice as a comedian. Our methods will include how to mine personal stories for material, how to write one-liners and premises, and how to use improvisation to generate ideas instead of staring down a blank page. Classes will also give students the lay-of-the-land on the NY comedy scene - from welcoming weekly open mics to the best shows to see pros in action. Every class will include stage time to try out original material in front of classmates. 7-Week Class: Sunday, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, July 19th - August 30th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 5:30 PM - 6:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Wednesday, September 2nd. 7:00pm. Eris MainStage. Teacher: Sara Hennessey Bio: Sara Hennessey is an acclaimed Toronto comedian who now lives in Brooklyn, New York. Sara has performed at Just For Laughs Montreal multiple times, and has 3 comedy albums available: Huge Bitch, They Know Too Much & Trouble in Saradise. Sara writes and performs for television when possible, and has a whirling affinity for live comedy & making indie projects with her incredibly talented friends. Testimonials: Truthfully I have been doing comedy but never took a stand up class. Sara helped me hone in on my "character" and speak more strongly in her voice. Also she's just so patient and excited with everyone which is very encouraging to witness and contagious. Sara couldn't have done a better job teaching this class! She struck a perfect balance between holding our hand in the early stages of the process while knowing when to push us and how much. She was kind and approached comedy without being pretentious or gatekeep-y. She was an open book and I appreciate her being candid to all sorts of questions I had—what do you do when you get nervous, how to deal with hecklers, etc. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.
