classes.title: Stand-Up Level 2: Solidifying Your Set w/ JoAnn Schinderle (July-August '26) (Mondays)
classes.description: Solidifying Your Set This class is limited to 12 students. Building on Level 1 "Finding Your Voice", Stand-up: Level 2 is all about solidifying your choices to develop your voice and find your niche. By focusing on each individual’s strengths, this course will focus on how to craft a clean set, host and put together a show, and develop tricks to solidify your own voice. We will workshop material as a group and I’ll give personalized feedback every week. If you want to take your sets to a deeper level and have a better understanding of what makes you funny, take this class. Class will culminate in a performance of each student’s 10-minute sets, leading you to the beginnings of your very own stand alone show. Prerequisite : Completion of BCC Level 1 Stand-Up OR students who have taken at least one full-length stand-up course at a major comedy theater (UCB, PIT, Magnet, Squirrel, Second City, iO, Annoyance, etc.) or have similar prior training or performance experience can begin at this level. Please write your prior experience in the form when registering. 7-Week Class: Monday, 7:00 PM - 10:00 PM, July 13th - August 24th, 2026 Note: Class may end between 9:30 PM - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. You will receive a welcome email with your room assignment. Our classes meet at BCC Pig Pen or BCC DogHouse in East Williamsburg. Class Show: Saturday, August 29th. 7:00pm. Eris MainStage. Class Show: Saturday, August 29th. 8:30pm. Eris MainStage. Teacher: JoAnn Schinderle Bio: JoAnn Schinderle is a Brooklyn-based standup, actress, and writer. Getting her start in Portland, OR she was voted PDX's Funniest Person by peers and industry, as well as an ‘Undisputable Genius of Comedy’ by local papers. A seasoned opener for touring acts including Fortune Feimster, Tom Papa, and Lena Dunham (‘14 book tour), JoAnn has also been featured nationally in festivals such as; SF Sketchfest, Bridgetown, Bumbershoot, B4UDIE, & 10,000 Laughs. In 2019, JoAnn wrote and produced an album of period jokes titled ‘Dark Jeans', as a middle finger to the notion that 'period jokes aren’t funny'. Turns out, they are? Dark Jeans sat #1 on iTunes during the week of its release with Comedy Dynamics. From there, she's a proud featured talking head on the 2024 MSNBC documentary 'Periodical' featuring Gloria Steinem. Schinderle serves her day job duties as a live audience host for network TV, Venue Manager for UCB LA (‘22-’23) & UCB NYC (‘23-24’), and her most favorite job of writing commissioned erotica under her not-yet-famous company, ‘JoJosNosNos’. Testimonials: Jo created a supportive and fun environment in which to explore ideas that feel too intimidating to try out in other stand up contexts! JoAnn is awesome! If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Start with Nothing, Leave with Something w/ Natasha Vaynblat (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 14 students. For festival tickets, please click here ! Do you love standup but have a hard time sitting down and putting pen to paper? This workshop is for you! Do you have a stand up set but are having a hard time extending it? This workshop is for you! Through a series of exercises students will learn how to generate standup material through improvisation. This class is a hybrid of writing and performing-- we will jump back and forth from being up on our feet to sitting down in front of the page. Beginners welcome! Prep: Come with jokes or come with nothing, just bring yourself and a willingness to get messy (metaphorically speaking). Workshop: Saturday, July 11th, 11:30 AM- 2 PM Where: BCC Dog House, Studio 1 (137 Montrose Ave) Teacher: Natasha Vaynblat Bio: Natasha Vaynblat’s comedy is “silly and searingly smart” (Paste Magazine). She has written for The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, A Little Late with Lilly Singh, and Comedy Central’s “Your Worst Fears Confirmed”. Her humor writing has appeared in The New Yorker, The Atlantic, and McSweeney's. Her standup album “We’re All Dads Here” was featured in Vulture's “Albums To Watch Right Now”. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Two Prov Tuneup w/ John Randall (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, please click here ! This class is for any two prov teams or any one person interested in learning how to navigate a two person improv set. The focus will be on having a dynamic show with only two performers on stage which includes playing multiple characters, editing from within, and having deep grounded scenes that keep the audience engaged. Workshop: Wednesday, July 8th, 7:00 PM - 9:30 PM Where: BCC DogHouse, Studio 1 (137 Montrose Ave) Teacher: John Randall Bio: John Randall is a comedian/actor/filmmaker based in Brooklyn. After getting his start as a standup in Boston, he moved to Chicago to study improv and sketch at The Second City, iO Chicago, and The Annoyance. He also trained as a clown at the Chicago Actors Gymnasium and studied Meisner at Green Shirt Studio. He has performed at the New York Comedy Festival, Flyover Comedy Festival, the Detroit Improv Festival and the Chicago Improv Festival. You can see his team The Letdown perform an hour of two-person improv monthly at the BCC, where he also teaches. Testimonials: John is a fantastic teacher! In his class, I have grown not only as an improv player but as an actor, and I would love to take another class with him in the future. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: What Are You Trying to Say: Setting Intentions in Improv w/ Anthony Atamanuik (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Workshop limited to 16 students. For festival tickets, please click here ! In this workshop with veteran improviser, Anthony Atamanuik, you’ll slow scene work down and discuss how to set intentions with each line. How do you feel about your scene partner? How do you feel about the scene? Everything your character says should clarify these questions, adding nuance and meaning to the scene. Expect side coaching in order to “stress test” the scene. Prerequisite: Completion of Level 1 Improv at any Major Comedy Theater Workshop: Sunday, July 12th, 3:00 PM - 5:30 PM Where: BCC Dog House, Studio 1 (137 Montrose Ave) Teacher: Anthony Atamanuik Bio: Anthony Atamanuik is known as the creator, executive producer, and star of Comedy Central’s THE PRESIDENT SHOW and its subsequent specials, A PRESIDENT SHOW DOCUMENTARY: THE FALL OF DONALD TRUMP and MAKE AMERICA GREAT-A-THON. Anthony has been performing and teaching improv at UCB since 2002 and is a member of the legendary improv group Death by Roo Roo. He can be seen recurring on the FX series WHAT WE DO IN THE SHADOWS as “Sean,” recurring on Adult Swim’s THREE BUSY DEBRAS, and his other TV credits include SEARCH PARTY, UNBREAKABLE KIMMY SCHMIDT, 30 ROCK, DIFFICULT PEOPLE, and BROAD CITY. Selected as one of Variety’s 2016 Comics to Watch, he has received critical acclaim and a fervent new fan base with his pitch-perfect Donald Trump impression. If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.

classes.title: Where the Hell Are We w/ John Lehr (FAD 2026 Workshop)
classes.description: Each class is limited to 16 students. ‍ ‍ For festival tickets, please click here ! Tired of standing on stage in the middle of nowhere? Or randomly making up a location because that’s what you are supposed to do? This workshop reinvigorates your muscles to find the “where” in a completely organic way. Afterwards, we take location into the stratosphere and discover how discovering our “where” leads to connectivity, character and relationships! Workshop: Saturday, July 11th, 2:15 PM - 4:45 PM Where: BCC Pig Pen, Studio B (130 Graham Ave) Teacher: John Lehr Bio: John Lehr is a comedic performer, writer and producer working in television, film and theater. Lehr starred as Leslie Pool in “ 10 Items or Less, ” from Sony on TBS for three seasons and recently starred as Sheriff Hoyle on Hulu’s original comedy western “ Quickdraw ” for two seasons. Lehr also co-created, wrote and executive produced both series. Lehr is one of the original Geico Cavemen from the wildly successful commercial campaign. He appeared in dozens of spots, including the first commercial as a caveman boom operator, the caveman in therapy with Talia Shire, the tennis spot with Billy Jean King, Superbowl spots with Phil Simms and many more. Under their banner Howler Monkey Productions , Lehr and producing partner Nancy Hower have created multiple projects, most involving their unique improvisationally-based “hybrid” style found in “ QuickDraw ” (Hulu) “ 10 Items ” (TBS) “ Jailbait (Crackle) and “ Memron ” (Slamdance award-winner). With Lehr starring and Howler directing, the team completed comedy pilots “Let It Ride,” (Comedy Central), “Retreat!” (NBC), “Team McPhearson (Fox) and “King of Beers” (EUE/Sokolow). The team’s script/development deals include “The Loop” (HBO), “Troubadour” (MTV), “Life on Mars” (Sony/BBC), and “LARP” (Echo Lake), Tommy Chong’s Pipe Dreams (TBS). If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here .

classes.title: Workshop: Don’t Call It Clown (Call It Character) w/ Deanna Fleysher aka Big D. (July 29th & 30th)
classes.description: Each class is limited to 20 students. Workshop: Don’t Call It Clown (Call It Character) (July 29th & 30th) an intensive workshop in character clown with Deanna Fleysher aka Big D from TV's "King Of Drag" Wanna be unforgettable? Connect with your audiences like never before! Make them love you forever! in this intensive workshop with a master of interactive/clown character comedy, you will embark on a journey based in clown and physical theater to birth and hone electrifying clown characters that audiences will love, rooted deeply in who you are. We will learn about your character clown's unique connection to the audience, their mission, and their special brand of vulnerable ferocity. In this intensive, expect the following! The beginning, or the deepening, of a clown character that is uniquely yours and unstoppably lovable, and actual material you can take out of this intensive and onto a stage Personalized feedback (and plenty of it) Discovery and exploration of your character's favorite games and physical/emotional "sweet spots" More understanding of what “stage magnetism” means for you, and how to really harness your performative power. A deeper connection to and understanding of your audience More wildness, more bravery, more vulnerability. Interpretive Dance. Laughing a lot. This workshop is ideal for performers of any kind, improvisers, clowns, stand-up comedians, actors, movers, and anyone who wants to find/hone their own magnetic, funny and brave persona to play through on stage. Two-Day Workshop: Wednesday, July 29th & Thursday, July 30th. 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM. Where: BCC Pig Pen Theater (144 Boerum Street, Brooklyn) Teacher: Deanna Fleysher Bio: ABOUT THE TEACHER: Deanna Fleysher aka Big D is a character comedian and clown with 20+ years experience in masculine impersonation and general idiocy. She has toured the world with her solo character clown show BUTT KAPINSKI , her new duo show CINEPRIXX , and her workshops in character, clown, and drag. She has directed/co-created over 30+ solo character clown shows, supporting artists from all over the world in finding and honing their own unstoppable characters. Most recently, she appeared on season 1 of KING OF DRAG , on Revry. She will eat your talent for breakfast. More at www.dontcallitclown.com If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here .

classes.title: [Virtual] Writing Your Comedy Pilot w/ Rich Talarico (Thursdays, July-Sep '26)
classes.description: This class is limited to 10 students. In this 7 session class, students will learn comedy pilot basics like pilot structure, character and story development, writers room skills like pitching, punch - ups, screenplay software and formatting. Students are not allowed to bring in old projects, but come to class “blank slate,” where e ach writer will create new characters and worlds culminating in a final read through of samples from e ach writers' pilots created during the class. Seven class sessions over 8 weeks (Skipping Aug 20). The writers room vibes will be elevated by participation from James Livingston, Hollywood script coo r dinator who will act as writers assistant to the class. Class will be led by Peabody Award winning and 4x Emmy nominated writer and producer, Rich Talarico (Key & Peele, SNL, Tonight S h ow, MADtv and Review). 7-Week Class: Thursdays, 7:00PM - 10:00 PM EST, July 16th - Sep 3 , 2026 Class may end between 9:30 - 10:00 PM depending on the number of enrollments. Class skips August 20th. This class is virtual. More information will be provided in your welcome email. Notes: Only sign up if you are able to make all classes. There are no makeup classes. This class does not end in a class show. Teacher: Rich Talarico Bio: Rich is an actor, comedian, improviser and established writer/producer best known for his work on Comedy Central’s Key & Peele. Rich wrote K&P favorites Substitute Teacher, Racist Zombies, Karim and Jahar, Hatz, Teaching Center, Obama Greets, Black Ice and many more. Rich wrote and produced on MADtv, The Tonight Show w. Jay Leno, Saturday Night Live, and the critically acclaimed, Review. Rich took his first improv class from Stephen Colbert at Chicago’s Second City. Rich went on to tour with Second City's National Touring Company and later co-created five original sketch comedy revues with Second City's Etc., and Main Stage Companies. While in Chicago, Rich was a performer and teacher at Improv Olympic (Now iO) under the direction of Charna Halpern and the late, Del Close. As an actor, Rich has appeared in featured roles on Late Night with Conan O’Brien, MADtv, the feature film High Fidelity, and A&W Root Beer’s classic TV ad, “Mr. Dumass.” Talarico’s extensive experience in the comedy industry has made him a sought-after teacher, writer and consultant. — More at richtalarico.com Testimonials: “Rich's work on "Key & Peele" is nothing short of legendary.” — Jordan Peele “As a teacher, Rich Talarico brings Lakers legendary coach Phil Jackson to mind. A cerebral educator who brings out the best in people.” — Keith “Lucky” Lehrer original drummer of “Circle Jerks” If this class is sold out, sign-up for the wait list here.
